name: The Pythagorean Theorem
whatIsIt: In a RIGHT-ANGLED triangle, the longest side (the HYPOTENUSE, opposite the right angle) is linked to the other two by a famous rule: a² + b² = c². Square the two shorter sides, add them, and that equals the square of the hypotenuse. To find a missing side, use squares and square roots.
whyNeeded: Pythagoras finds distances you can't measure directly — the length of a ramp, a ladder against a wall, the diagonal of a screen, or the straight-line distance on a map. It's one of the most used rules in construction, navigation and physics.
realLifeUses: How long a ladder reaches up a wall
The diagonal length of a TV or phone screen
The straight-line distance between two points
Checking a corner is a true right angle (3-4-5 rule)

vocabulary.meaning: The longest side of a right triangle, opposite the right angle.
The Pythagorean rule: the two short sides squared add to the hypotenuse squared.

story.title: The Leaning Ladder
story.text: Uncle's ladder stands 4 m up the wall, its foot 3 m out. 'How long is the ladder?' asks Vikram. He uses Pythagoras: 3² + 4² = 9 + 16 = 25, and √25 = 5. 'The ladder is 5 metres long!' Uncle grins — 'the 3-4-5 triangle, the builder's favourite.'
story.extractedProblem: A ladder reaches 4 m up a wall with its foot 3 m out. How long is the ladder?

visual.caption: Right triangle: 3² + 4² = 9 + 16 = 25 = 5². The hypotenuse (longest side) is 5.

teachingGallery.title: Square, add, root
teachingGallery.note: a² + b² = c²; the hypotenuse is the longest side.

examples.caption: Square a side by making a square grid.

standardMethod.summary: Identify the right angle and hypotenuse; use a² + b² = c², squaring and taking roots to find the missing side.
steps: Find the right angle; the side opposite it is the hypotenuse (longest).
To find the hypotenuse: square the two short sides, add, then take the square root.
To find a short side: subtract the known short side's square from the hypotenuse's square, then take the root.
Check: the hypotenuse must be the longest side.

workedExamples.problem: A right triangle has short sides 6 and 8. Find the hypotenuse.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 13 and one short side 5. Find the other short side.

commonMistakes.mistake: Adding the sides instead of their squares (3 + 4 = 7 as the hypotenuse).
commonMistakes.fix: Square first: 3² + 4² = 25, THEN take the root → 5. Not 3 + 4.

commonMistakes.mistake: Treating a short side as the hypotenuse.
commonMistakes.fix: The hypotenuse is always the LONGEST side, opposite the right angle. It goes on the c² side of the equation.

easy.q: In a right triangle, 3² + 4² = ? (write the total, like 25)
The hypotenuse whose square is 25 is √25 = ?

medium.q: Right triangle short sides 6 and 8 — hypotenuse?

challenge.q: Hypotenuse 10, one short side 6 — the other short side?
Ravi says a triangle with short sides 3 and 4 has hypotenuse 7. What's the fix?

questions.q: Hypotenuse 13, short side 12 — the other short side?
The hypotenuse of a right triangle is…

options.label: The longest side, opposite the right angle

questions.answer: The longest side, opposite the right angle

teachBackPrompt: Make a 3-4-5 triangle with string or a set square and show a grown-up that 3² + 4² = 5². Explain how Pythagoras finds a ladder's length or a screen's diagonal — square the two sides, add, then take the square root.

revisionCard.summary: Right-angled triangle: a² + b² = c², where c is the hypotenuse (longest side, opposite the 90°). Find the hypotenuse: square the short sides, add, root. Find a short side: hypotenuse² − known² , then root. Square first — never just add the sides.
